title: Identify line symmetry
description: Practice identifying lines of symmetry on shapes and determining whether a shape has line symmetry.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following shapes show a line of symmetry?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###What is a line of symmetry? 

A line of symmetry is a line where we can fold the image and have both halves match exactly.
When we divide the following figure with the line, the resulting two parts are  **not** mirror images of each other:



[[☃ image 1]]

This figure does **not** show a line of symmetry. 
When we divide these figures with the line, the resulting two parts are mirror images of each other:


[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]

These figures show lines of symmetry.
The following figures show lines of symmetry:

 | |
- | - | -

[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ image 2]]

choices.content: ![A line passes through the left and right points of a rhombus.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d823701f9f7a9f2abefcbf946330207b98acb8a4)
![A line passes through the base and top point of a triangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1bdba2584978feaa54ff3e623af0e034e333caca)
![A line passes through the top left corner and bottom right corner of a rectangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/06b0bc5ab2317134a7211de48655c0a8774a2ae8)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following shapes show a line of symmetry?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###What is a line of symmetry? 

A line of symmetry is a line where we can fold the image and have both halves match exactly.

When we divide the following figure with the line, the resulting two parts are  **not** mirror images of each other:

![A vertical line passes through opposite sides of a rhombus.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9e3eb7cd1ad922f70215118bfba37ffb96816c70)

This figure does **not** show a line of symmetry.

When we divide these figures with the line, the resulting two parts are mirror images of each other:

![A line passes through the base and opposite point of a pentagon.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ea546a85177caf3f636277bb81a7b1f5061160e4)

![A line passes through the top left corner and bottom right corner of a square.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0f3ba9c487452b3a55454c327bbfebb54d4076b6)

These figures show lines of symmetry.

The following figures show lines of symmetry:

 | |
- | - | -
![Two figures are shown.  In the first figure a line passes through the base and opposite point of a pentagon. In the second figure a line passes through the top left corner and bottom right corner of a square.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ea546a85177caf3f636277bb81a7b1f5061160e4) | ![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0f3ba9c487452b3a55454c327bbfebb54d4076b6)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following shapes show a line of symmetry?** 


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###What is a line of symmetry? 

A line of symmetry is a line where we can fold the image and have both halves match exactly.

When we divide the following figures with the line, the resulting two parts are  **not** mirror images of each other:

![A line passes through the middle of one side and the opposite point of an irregular pentagon.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bf6b16c6eb29163aeae691a8a3a6e76784ccbf3f)

![A line passes through the base and top point of a triangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5c654959e469d9221bf0044ed999b3fd1d39c89b)

These figures do **not** show lines of symmetry.

When we divide this figure with the line, the resulting two parts are mirror images of each other:

![A line passes through the middle of the parallel sides of a trapezoid.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/393ca523916a2468399b5ea3bc233f5c5df44720)

This figure shows line of symmetry.

The following figure shows a line of symmetry:

![A line passes through the middle of the parallel sides of a trapezoid.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/393ca523916a2468399b5ea3bc233f5c5df44720)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which shapes have line symmetry?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A $\greenD{\text{line of symmetry}}$ is an imaginary line where the image can be folded to make two halves that match exactly, like mirror images. 

A shape has $\purpleD{\text{line symmetry}}$ if it has at least one line of symmetry.

We can draw at least one $\greenD{\text{line of symmetry}}$ on the following shape:

![A hexagon with a line passing through the middle of opposite sides and a line passing through two points that are opposite.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4fdd9d009d8b8414bfe535865d0cb3360343dc5c)

So, this shape has $\purpleD{\text{line symmetry}}$.  

![A hexagon.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4547a59bc0a312e70f0e189a95e233c464a656d1)

choices.content: ![An irregular hexagon with no equal sides.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5335368d3d22673d067194b3afd2887220629c12)
![A hexagon.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4547a59bc0a312e70f0e189a95e233c464a656d1)

![A triangular-like shape that has a line that starts at the top and moves down and right, then at the end moves down and to the left, then up and slightly to the right. The line then continues down and left, then continues up and slightly right to the beginning of the first line.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/46447240bec90186d45f819aae135b6342df86d6)

hints:
A $\greenD{\text{line of symmetry}}$ is an imaginary line where the image can be folded to make two halves that match exactly, like mirror images. 

A shape has $\purpleD{\text{line symmetry}}$ if it has at least one line of symmetry.

We can draw at least one $\greenD{\text{line of symmetry}}$ on the following shape:

![A pentagon with two lines of symmetry. Each line of symmetry passes through the middle of one side and the opposite point.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/38bf890393099044518b5ccd45026dc8e54cf310)

So, this shape has $\purpleD{\text{line symmetry}}$.  

![A pentagon.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6a6115475d4263be30148ea94313d32f64911829)

choices.content: ![A triangular-like shape that has a line that starts at the top and moves down and right, then at the end moves down and to the left, then up and slightly to the right. The line then continues down and left, then continues up and slightly right to the beginning of the first line.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/60722cdb018e7890f7223ab6b0187fc517288a32)
![A quadrilateral with no equal sides.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1ae1093581e8176d2d5b65143d7ef17020369483)
![A pentagon.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6a6115475d4263be30148ea94313d32f64911829)

So, this shape has $\purpleD{\text{line symmetry}}$.

hints:
A $\greenD{\text{line of symmetry}}$ is an imaginary line where the image can be folded to make two halves that match exactly, like mirror images. 

A shape has $\purpleD{\text{line symmetry}}$ if it has at least one line of symmetry.

We can draw at least one $\greenD{\text{line of symmetry}}$ on the following shapes:

![A kite-shaped quadrilateral with two pairs of adjacent equal sides. A line of symmetry passes through the points between the adjacent sides of equal length.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9b746a3182ff140cb83865c1b32cbfc5592a3a16)

![A triangle with two equal sides with a line of symmetry passing through the middle of the long side and the opposite side.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/603852bc62879deaefb91b194c65a8d9bf62a24c)

So, these shapes have $\purpleD{\text{line symmetry}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which shapes have line symmetry?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A $\greenD{\text{line of symmetry}}$ is an imaginary line where the image can be folded to make two halves that match exactly, like mirror images. 

A shape has $\purpleD{\text{line symmetry}}$ if it has at least one line of symmetry.

We can draw at least one $\greenD{\text{line of symmetry}}$ on the following shapes:

![A triangle with three equal sides.  Three lines of symmetry pass through the middle of each side and the opposite points.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ec012e56b25c87741e29d010df13a12e72cf8ceb)

![A rectangle with two lines of symmetry.  One line passes through the middle of the left and right sides. The other line passes through the middle of the top and bottom sides.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a43fbee9c96c0572d2021e5a517db1c499bf23a9)

So, these shapes have $\purpleD{\text{line symmetry}}$.  

![A triangle with three sides of equal length.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1077a6dbb74ad271736c6e5e4638ac441d4a45b8)

![A rectangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4854deb6505fa225ef6740461931ba295b7db584)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which shapes are symmetrical?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A $\greenD{\text{line of symmetry}}$ is an imaginary line where the image can be folded to make two halves that match exactly, like mirror images. 

A shape has $\purpleD{\text{line symmetry}}$ if it has at least one line of symmetry.

We can draw at least one $\greenD{\text{line of symmetry}}$ on the following shapes:

![A hexagon with all sides equal has three lines of symmetry.  Each line passes through opposite sides.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/18f700ae6fb9e8abcbbae4c21b6cdbca4680b18e)

![A triangle with equal sides has three lines of symmetry.  Each line passes through a side and the opposite point.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ec012e56b25c87741e29d010df13a12e72cf8ceb)

![A hexagon with two pairs of equal sides and two unequal sides. The equal sides are not adjacent. A line passes through the sides on the top and bottom.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/64a1c4216d88f3e0f70686cf2154eee2a2e253d4)

So, these shapes have $\purpleD{\text{line symmetry}}$.  

![A hexagon.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4547a59bc0a312e70f0e189a95e233c464a656d1)

![A triangle with three sides of equal length.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1077a6dbb74ad271736c6e5e4638ac441d4a45b8)

![A hexagon with two pairs of equal sides and two unequal sides. The equal sides are not adjacent.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0280dc568b325137cf0d2833da52f82508e100db)

choices.content: ![A hexagon.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4547a59bc0a312e70f0e189a95e233c464a656d1)
![A triangle with all sides of equal length.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1077a6dbb74ad271736c6e5e4638ac441d4a45b8)
![A hexagon with two pairs of equal sides and two unequal sides. The equal sides are not adjacent.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0280dc568b325137cf0d2833da52f82508e100db)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Draw a line of symmetry through the triangle by connecting two black points.**

[[☃ interactive-graph 1]]
hints:
A line of symmetry is a line where we can fold the image and have both halves match exactly.


How can we position the line so each half is a mirror image of the other?

The line of symmetry should look like this: 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a07b50f3bf22a004f9e65334e89f4e6781a6d48f)
